evidence: Repo root `package.json` is `private:true`, package name `kolmogorov-stack`, and exposes bin `kolm: cli/kolm.js`.
risk: Docs that advertise published package names do not match the root package metadata.
recommended_action: Pick canonical install path for launch: GitHub install, npm package, or both; align package metadata and docs.

evidence: `node --check cli/kolm.js` passed and `node cli/kolm.js version` executed successfully.
risk: The canonical CLI file is syntactically valid and has a working version command in this environment.
recommended_action: Keep root CLI as canonical while SDK/package docs are cleaned up.

evidence: README and docs advertise `@kolmogorov/kolm`, `@kolm/cli`, and `github:sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`; root package is private and named `kolmogorov-stack`.
risk: Developers may try non-existent or unpublished package names before reaching the working GitHub install path.
recommended_action: Remove unpublished names or publish matching packages before public launch.

evidence: `node --check` failed for `public/sdk.js` and all versioned `public/sdk-*.js` files at line 18 with `Unexpected token ':'`.
risk: P0 developer-surface failure: the advertised `import { recipe } from 'https://kolm.ai/sdk.js'` cannot load.
recommended_action: Fix syntax, add `node --check public/sdk.js` and browser import smoke to CI, then rebuild versioned SDK assets.

evidence: `public/sdk-current.json` points to `sdk-c102c349da28.js`, which also fails syntax check.
risk: The immutable/SRI manifest pins a broken asset, so consumers following best-practice pinning still fail.
recommended_action: Rebuild `sdk-current.json` only after syntax and runtime smoke pass.

evidence: Browser SDK comments claim public registry runs locally and returns signed receipts; the code builds local hash receipts and has no HMAC/public-key signature path.
risk: Even after syntax is fixed, receipt wording can overstate cryptographic guarantees.
recommended_action: Call browser receipts local run attestations unless backed by signed public-key or verifiable HMAC flow.

evidence: `sdk/node/package.json` is `@kolmogorov/kolm-sdk`, with a recipe-named CLI bin, and README says the npm package is not published yet.
risk: Node SDK is coherent as local package, but not a published install path.
recommended_action: Publish or clearly label as local-only; avoid presenting it as installed package in public docs.

evidence: `node --check` passed for `sdk/node/index.mjs`, `index.cjs`, and `bin/cli.mjs`.
risk: Node SDK sources are syntactically valid.
recommended_action: Add mocked unit tests plus route contract tests.

evidence: Mocked `recipe.isSpam()` called `/v1/public/featured`, then unauthenticated `/v1/run`, which returned 401.
risk: Public no-key convenience helpers do not work; they should call `/v1/public/run` with the found concept id.
recommended_action: Change shortcuts and MCP named runs to use `publicRun({concept_id})` for curated public recipes.

evidence: `node sdk/node/test/sdk.test.mjs` failed 0 pass / 9 fail when no server was listening on localhost:3939.
risk: SDK tests are live-server smokes, not reliable package tests; they also do not catch the public shortcut bug by default.
recommended_action: Add fetch-mocked unit tests and make live tests opt-in with a clear env variable.

evidence: `sdk/mcp/package.json` depends on a legacy recipe-scoped package name, while the repo's Node SDK package is `@kolmogorov/kolm-sdk`; a nested legacy package copy exists under sdk/mcp.
risk: MCP package may not install from a clean checkout or registry because its dependency name is not the local package name.
recommended_action: Unify package names or add a real workspace/package for the dependency.

evidence: MCP `recipe_run` finds a curated recipe by name, then calls `client.run`, which maps to protected `/v1/run`.
risk: No-key MCP public recipe runs will fail unless the MCP server has an API key, contradicting public curated recipe positioning.
recommended_action: Use public run for curated recipes and authenticated run only for private ids.

evidence: `sdk/python/pyproject.toml` publishes project name `kolm` but only script `recipe`; README title and install command use a legacy recipe PyPI name.
risk: Python users cannot tell whether they should install `kolm`, the legacy recipe PyPI name, or use editable local mode.
recommended_action: Split packages or rename docs/scripts to one canonical package identity.

evidence: `sdk/python/recipe/client.py` defaults to the legacy Railway production host; README signup uses the same old host.
risk: Python recipe flows can bypass `kolm.ai` and hit stale infrastructure or fail if Railway host diverges.
recommended_action: Default to `https://kolm.ai` and keep old host only as explicit override.

evidence: `RecipeClient.synthesize_batch` posts `{recipes:[...]}` while server `/v1/synthesize/batch` expects `{items:[...]}`.
risk: Batch synthesis through Python sends the wrong request shape.
recommended_action: Change Python client to `{items}` and add contract tests.

evidence: `RecipeClient.verify` posts `{source, examples}` while server `/v1/verify` expects `{source, positives, negatives}`.
risk: Python verify examples are not passed to the verifier as intended.
recommended_action: Map examples to positives or expose positives/negatives parameters.

evidence: `RecipeClient.public_run` sends `{name}` or `{recipe_id}`; server `/v1/public/run` accepts `concept_id` or `version_id`.
risk: Python public shortcuts and CLI named runs cannot work against the current public-run endpoint.
recommended_action: Resolve names via `/v1/public/featured` or `/v1/public/concepts`, then call public run with `concept_id`.

evidence: `Kolm.compile` fallback posts `examples_uri` and `base`, then expects response `id`; server expects `examples` and `base_model`, returns `job_id`.
risk: Python compile fallback breaks when the Node CLI is absent.
recommended_action: Update request/response mapping to current `/v1/compile` contract.

evidence: `Kolm.compile` shells out with `--base`, `--json`, `--recall`, and `--recipe-pack-depth`; root CLI cloud compile supports `--base-model`, `--data`, `--examples`, `--out`, and `--deploy-hook`.
risk: Python CLI bridge can silently ignore or fail flags, so Python compile is unreliable even with Node CLI installed.
recommended_action: Update flags and parse the actual CLI output or use HTTP only.

evidence: No `python` or `py` executable is available in this environment, so Python files could not be byte-compiled locally.
risk: Syntax and packaging health for Python SDK remain unverified in this workspace.
recommended_action: Run Python CI in an environment with Python 3.8+ and add mocked HTTP tests.

evidence: `public/docs.html` footer says docs v0.7.8 while package/CLI versions are 0.1.0 and server health reports 0.2.0.
risk: Version numbers are confusing and undermine developer trust.
recommended_action: Define separate docs/site/API/CLI versions or remove stale displayed version.

evidence: Docs mention commands such as `kolm tune --airgap`, `kolm rag attach`, `kolm install --apply`, and package installs that may be preview or partially implemented.
risk: Developers cannot tell which flows are stable, preview, local-only, or aspirational.
recommended_action: Add command maturity labels and smoke-tested quickstart paths.
